passages.title: The History of Pencil
passages.source_html:
The History of Pencil 


A 


The beginning of the story of pencils started with lightning. Graphite, the main material for producing a pencil, was discovered in 1564 in Borrowdale in England when lightning struck a local tree during a thunder. Local people found out that the black substance spotted at the root of the unlucky tree was different from burning ash of wood. It was soft, thus left marks everywhere. The chemistry was barely out of its infancy at the time, so people mistook it for lead, equally black but much heavier. It was soon put to use by locals in marking their sheep for signs of ownership and calculation. 


B 


Britain turns out to be the major country where mines of graphite can be detected and developed. Even so, the first pencil was invented elsewhere. As graphite is soft, it requires some form of the encasement. In Italy, graphite sticks were initially wrapped in string or sheepskin for stability, becoming perhaps the very first pencil in the world. Then around 1560, an Italian couple made what are likely the first blueprints for the modern, wood-encased carpentry pencil their version was a flat, oval, more compact type of pencil. Their concept involved the hollowing out of a stick of juniper wood. Shortly thereafter in 1662, a superior technique was discovered by German people: two wooden halves were carved, a graphite stick inserted, and the halves then glued together – essentially the same method in use to this day. The news of usefulness of these early pencils spread far and wide, attracting the attention of artists all over the known world. 


C 


Although graphite core in pencils is still referred to as lead, modern pencils do not contain lead as the ‘lead’ of the pencil is actually a mix of finely ground graphite and clay powders. This mixture is important because the amount of clay content added to the graphite depends on intended pencil hardness, and the amount of time spent on grinding the mixture determines the quality of the lead. The more clay you put in, the higher hardness the core has. Many pencils across the world and almost all in Europe are graded on the European system. This system of naming used B for black and H for hard; a pencil’s grade was described by a sequence or successive Hs or Bs such as BB and BBB for successively softer leads, and HH and HHH for successively harder ones. Then the standard writing pencil is graded HB. 


D 


In England, pencils continue to be made from whole sawn graphite. But with the mass production of pencils, they are getting drastically more popular in many countries with each passing decade. As demands rise, appetite for graphite soars. 


E


According to the United States Geological Survey (USGS), world production of natural graphite in 2012 was 1,100,000 tonnes, of which the following major exporters are: China, India, Brazil, North Korea and Canada. When the value of graphite was realised, the mines were taken over by the government and guarded. One of its chiefs uses during the reign of Elizabeth I in the second half of the 16th century was as moulds for the manufacture of cannonballs. Graphite was transported from Keswick to London in armed stagecoaches. In 1751 an Act of Parliament was passed making it an offence to steal or receive ‘wad’. This crime was punishable by hard labour or transportation. 


F


That the United States did not use pencils in the outer space till they spent $1,000 to make a pencil to use in zero gravity conditions is, in fact, a fiction. It is widely known that astronauts in Russia used grease pencils, which don’t have breakage problems. But it is also a fact that their counterparts in the United States used pencils in the outer space before real zero gravity pencil was invented. They preferred mechanical pencils, which produced fine lines, much clearer than the smudgy lines left by the grease pencils the Russians favoured. But the lead tips of these mechanical pencils broke often. That bit of graphite floating around the space capsule could get into someone’s eye, or even find its way into machinery or electronics, causing an electrical short or other problems. But despite the fact that the Americans did invent zero gravity pencils later, they stuck to mechanical pencils for many years. 


G 


Against the backcloth of a digitalized world, the prospect of pencils seems bleak. In reality, it does not. The application of pencils has by now become so widespread that they can be seen everywhere, such as classrooms, meeting rooms and art rooms, etc. A spectrum of users are likely to continue to use it into the future: students to do math works, artists to draw on sketch pads, waiters or waitresses to mark on order boards, make-up professional to apply to faces, and architects to produce blueprints. The possibilities seem limitless.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-19


Complete the sentences below. Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 14-19 on your answer sheet.


14

Graphite was found under a 14 in Borrowdale.

正确答案： 14 tree##Tree
Question 14

原文答案句：Graphite, the main material for producing a pencil, was discovered in 1564 in Borrowdale in England when lightning struck a local tree during a thunder.  

对应段落：A段  

审题：题目问石墨是在 Borrowdale 的一棵什么下面被发现的。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的专有名词 "Borrowdale" 和 "graphite" 定位到 A 段第一句，找到石墨被发现的地点和时间。  

解题思路：原文提到石墨是在 Borrowdale 的一棵树被闪电击中后发现的，因此石墨是在树（tree）下被发现的。答案为 tree。


15

It was dirty to use because it was 15 .

正确答案： 15 soft##Soft
Question 15

原文答案句：It was soft, thus left marks everywhere.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目要求完成句子，说明该物质（石墨）使用起来很脏的原因。

定位关键信息：使用 "dirty to use" 定位到段落A中描述石墨物理特性及其使用后果的句子。

解题思路：原文在描述刚发现的石墨时，明确指出它“质地柔软”，因此“到处留下痕迹”。正是这种“柔软”的特性，导致它在接触物体时会留下印记，从而显得“脏”。因此，答案是“soft”。


16

Ancient people used graphite to sign 16 .

正确答案： 16 sheep##Sheep
Question 16

原文答案句：It was soon put to use by locals in marking their sheep for signs of ownership and calculation.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问古代人用石墨来标记什么。

定位关键信息：根据题干中的"sign possession and number"定位到A段中"signs of ownership and calculation"这一部分。

解题思路：原文明确提到当地人使用石墨来标记他们的"sheep"（羊）以表示所有权，也便于清点。题干中的"sign possession and number"对应原文的"signs of ownership and calculation"，因此答案为sheep。


17

People found graphite 17 in Britain.

正确答案： 17 mines##Mines
Question 17

原文答案句：Britain turns out to be the major country where mines of graphite can be detected and developed.

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目要求完成句子，人们在英国发现了石墨的什么。  

定位关键信息：使用 "graphite", "Britain" 定位到描述英国石墨存在形式的句子。  

解题思路：原文明确指出，英国是拥有可勘探与可开采石墨矿藏的主要国家。这里的“mines”（矿藏）直接说明了石墨在英国是以“矿”的形式存在于地下的。因此，答案是“mines”。


18

The first pencil was graphite wrapped in 18 or animal skin.

正确答案： 18 string##String
Question 18

原文答案句：In Italy, graphite sticks were initially wrapped in string or sheepskin for stability, becoming perhaps the very first pencil in the world.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是第一支铅笔，是用什么来包裹石墨的。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"first pencil"和"graphite wrapped in"定位到B段中描述第一支铅笔的句子。

解题思路：根据原文B段句子"graphite sticks were initially wrapped in string or sheepskin for stability"可知，石墨棒最初是用string（绳子）或sheepskin（羊皮）包裹的，题干中已经提到sheepskin，因此空格处应填string。题目要求填一个单词，且原文中是string，因此答案为string。


19

Russian astronauts preferred 19 pencils to write in the outer space.

正确答案： 19 grease##Grease
Question 19

原文答案句：It is widely known that astronauts in Russia used grease pencils, which don’t have breakage problems.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是俄罗斯宇航员在太空中更喜欢使用什么类型的铅笔。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"Russian astronauts"和"pencils to write in the outer space"可以定位到E段中关于俄罗斯宇航员使用铅笔的描述。

解题思路：原文中明确提到"astronauts in Russia used grease pencils"，即俄罗斯宇航员使用了油性铅笔。因此，答案为grease。


Questions 20-26


Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the passage? In boxes 20-26 on your answer sheet, write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

 


20

Italy is probably the first country in the whole world to make pencils.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：In Italy, graphite sticks were initially wrapped in string or sheepskin for stability, becoming perhaps the very first pencil in the world.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问“意大利可能是世界上第一个制作铅笔的国家”，需要判断这一说法是否与原文一致。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“Italy”和“first country to make pencils”定位到B段首句。  

解题思路：此处谈到意大利最早制作铅笔的方法，并说“这也许是世界上的第一支铅笔”。再往前看，发现在第2段第2句谈到“first pencil”是在别的（英国以外的）地方被发明的。再依顺序看到第4、5句，根据文意发现这个所谓的“别的地方（elsewhere）”指的就是意大利。因此题目句对应原文，本题正确答案为TRUE。


21

Germany used various kinds of wood to make pencils.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Shortly thereafter in 1662, a superior technique was discovered by German people: two wooden halves were carved, a graphite stick inserted, and the halves then glued together – essentially the same method in use to this day. 

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是“德国使用多种木材制作铅笔”这一说法是否与文章内容一致。

定位关键信息：根据题目的关键词“Germany”和“various kinds of wood”定位到文章B段。

解题思路：在B段中，文章提到德国人在1662年发现了一种更先进的制作铅笔的技术，谈到了木材，但并未提及使用了多种木材。因此，文章中没有足够的信息来支持或反驳“德国使用多种木材制作铅笔”这一说法，所以答案为NOT GIVEN。


22

Graphite makes a pencil harder and sharper.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Although graphite core in pencils is still referred to as lead, modern pencils do not contain lead as the ‘lead’ of the pencil is actually a mix of finely ground graphite and clay powders....The more clay you put in, the higher hardness the core has. 

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问石墨是否能让铅笔更硬更尖。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"Graphite"和"harder and sharper"定位到C段描述铅笔芯的句子。  

解题思路：根据变硬（harder）定位到第3段第3句，发现使铅笔芯变硬的是黏土（clay）。再回到第3段第1句，发现铅笔芯内只包含石墨和黏土两种物质。则可得出结论为“石墨并不是那个使得铅笔芯变硬的物质”，故而题目与原文概念相矛盾，本题正确答案为FALSE。


23

Pencils are not produced any more since the reign of Elizabeth I.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：In England, pencils continued to be made from whole sawn graphite.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问“自从伊丽莎白一世统治时期后，铅笔就不再生产了”这一说法是否与原文一致。  

定位关键信息：通过“reign of Elizabeth I”和“not produced any more”定位到D段，该段提到“pencils continued to be made from whole sawn graphite”，说明铅笔仍在生产。  

解题思路：注意到“continued to be made”，表示英国仍然在继续制造铅笔。这与题目概念“not produced any more（不再制造）”相矛盾，故本题正确答案为FALSE。


24

The pencil was used during the first American space expedition.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：But it is also a fact that their counterparts in the United States used pencils in the outer space before real zero gravity pencil was invented. They preferred mechanical pencils, which produced fine lines, much clearer than the smudgy lines left by the grease pencils the Russians favoured.  

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问“美国人在第一次太空探索中使用了铅笔”，需要判断文章是否明确提到这一信息。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的“first American space expedition”定位到E段。  

解题思路：F段指出美国宇航员在零重力铅笔发明前使用过铅笔（mechanical pencils），但并未说明这是在“第一次太空探险”期间。文章未提供关于“第一次太空探险”的具体信息，因此无法确认题目所述内容是否属实。答案应为NOT GIVEN。


25

American astronauts did not replace mechanical pencils immediately after the zero-gravity pencils were invented.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：But despite the fact that the Americans did invent zero gravity pencils later, they stuck to mechanical pencils for many years.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“美国宇航员在零重力铅笔发明后，没有立即替换机械铅笔”，需要判断文章是否明确提到这一信息。

定位关键信息：题目中的“American astronauts”、“zero-gravity pencils were invented”和“did not replace mechanical pencils immediately”是定位的关键信息。通过这些关键词，可以在F段中找到对应的句子。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子“Despite the fact that the Americans did invent zero gravity pencils later, they stuck to mechanical pencils for many years.”，可以得出结论，美国宇航员在零重力铅笔被发明后，仍然继续使用机械铅笔很多年。这与题目中的陈述“American astronauts did not replace mechanical pencils immediately after the zero-gravity pencils were invented.”一致，因此答案是A. True。



26

Pencils are unlikely to be used in the future.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：The application of pencils has by now become so widespread that they can be seen everywhere, such as classrooms, meeting rooms and art rooms, etc. A spectrum of users are likely to continue to use it into the future: students to do math works, artists to draw on sketch pads, waiters or waitresses to mark on order boards, make-up professional to apply to faces, and architects to produce blueprints.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问“未来人们不太可能使用铅笔”，需要判断文章是否明确提到这一信息。

定位关键信息：通过题目的核心词“unlikely to be used in the future”定位到F段，该段明确提到铅笔的广泛应用和未来使用者。

解题思路：G段指出铅笔的应用已经非常广泛，并且列出了多种未来使用者（如学生、艺术家、服务员等），说明铅笔在未来仍会被继续使用，这与题目的说法直接矛盾。因此，题目与原文信息不符，答案为B（False）。
